page: Amazon Web Services d3.8xlarge is a dense storage instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 32 virtual CPUs, representing 16 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. A key hardware characteristic is its bundled local storage, consisting of 47,520 GB of HDD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the d3.8xlarge delivers average multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. However, single-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% in stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. Memory bandwidth is average across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES.

Given its massive 47.5 TB HDD storage capacity and average multi-core processing capabilities, this instance is designed for storage-intensive workloads. It is highly suitable for large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems, and big data analytics jobs that require high-density local storage rather than high-speed SSD IOPS. The qualitative cost efficiency of the d3.8xlarge is centered on its high storage-to-resource density, offering a massive amount of local HDD space per virtual core, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded compute speed.
description: Amazon Web Services d3.8xlarge is a dense storage instance built on the AWS Nitro System, featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 32 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes a massive 47,520 GB of bundled local HDD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth, contrasted by weak single-core performance. This hardware profile makes the d3.8xlarge highly suitable for storage-intensive workloads such as distributed file systems, data warehousing, and big data analytics. Its qualitative cost efficiency lies in its high storage density per virtual core, though users must trade off single-threaded processing speed for this high-capacity local HDD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3.8xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 47,520 GB of local HDD storage, optimized for large-scale data warehousing and distributed file systems.

tagline: A high-density storage instance featuring thirty-two virtual cores, DDR4 memory, and over forty-seven terabytes of local HDD capacity.

Features 47,520 GB of bundled local HDD storage
Average multi-core performance with weak single-core speeds
Best suited for distributed file systems and data warehousing
